
### data hazard compiler solution  {.smaller}


```{.gnuassembler style="font-size: 150%"}
addq %r8, %r9
nop
nop
addq %r9, %r8

```
 

* one solution: <em>change the ISA</em> 

   * all `addq`s take effect <em>three instructions later</em> <br> [(assuming can read register value while it is being written back)]{.fragment fragment-index=2 .custom .myem-only}

* make it <em>compiler's  job</em>
* problem: recompile everytime processor changes?


### stalling/nop pipeline diagram (1) 



::: {.r-stack .my-full}
![](/pipeline/texfig/addqHazardSoln-stallingnop-pipeline-diagram-1.figure-1.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=1}

![](/pipeline/texfig/addqHazardSoln-stallingnop-pipeline-diagram-1.figure-2.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=2}


:::


### stalling/nop pipeline diagram (2) 



::: {.r-stack .my-full}
![](/pipeline/texfig/addqHazardSoln-stallingnop-pipeline-diagram-2.figure-1.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=1}

![](/pipeline/texfig/addqHazardSoln-stallingnop-pipeline-diagram-2.figure-2.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=2}


:::


### data hazard hardware solution  {.smaller}


```
addq %r8, %r9
// hardware inserts: nop
// hardware inserts: nop
addq %r9, %r8

```
 

* how about hardware add nops?
* called <em>stalling</em>
* extra logic: 

   * sometimes don't change PC
   * sometimes put do-nothing values in pipeline registers
